title: Script Kit QA scenario picker
description: Select the Script Kit QA scenarios you want the local agent to run next. Each option includes the likely surface, risk, and starting DevTools proof commands.

options.title: Nested actions submenu is misanchored
options.description: A nested submenu appears a few pixels away from the parent row and can overlap the item that opened it.
options.goal: Prove the child route is anchored to the intended parent action row from the same layout generation.
options.risk: User chooses the wrong action because visual placement and logical parent diverge.

expectedEvidence.pass: Child menu origin matches parent action anchor within tolerance.
expectedEvidence.fail: Child menu uses stale or container-level anchor bounds.

options.title: Disabled action shortcut still fires
options.description: A disabled action still displays a keycap and pressing that shortcut runs the action.
options.goal: Compare model disabled state, visual disabled state, semantic disabled state, and accelerator binding state.
options.risk: Unavailable or unsafe commands can execute from a disabled row.

expectedEvidence.pass: Disabled row has no active accelerator and keypress dispatches no command.
expectedEvidence.fail: enabled=false but accelerator remains active or dispatches.

options.title: Tall actions menu clips bottom rows
options.description: A long actions menu grows beyond the window and bottom keycaps are partly cut off.
options.goal: Identify viewport, menu visible rect, scroll container, clipped rows, and keyboard reachability.
options.risk: Actions are visible enough to focus but not readable or clickable.

expectedEvidence.pass: Overflow is scrollable and focused rows can be fully revealed.
expectedEvidence.fail: Focusable rows or keycaps lie outside the visible menu rect.

options.title: Fast navigation opens stale nested action route
options.description: Moving quickly between actions opens a submenu for the previous row while a different row is highlighted.
options.goal: Trace requested parent item, highlighted item, route stack, and stale route cancellation.
options.risk: Child actions belong to the wrong visible parent.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/events.ts record --session <session> -- bun scripts/devtools/actions.ts inspect --session <session> --start --keep-open --open

expectedEvidence.pass: Visible child route parent id equals highlighted/focused parent id.
expectedEvidence.fail: Child route requestedParentItemId differs from highlightedItemId.

options.title: Action keycaps overlap long labels after resize
options.description: Late-arriving long labels widen the menu but keycaps stay at old x positions and overlap text.
options.goal: Measure row label bounds, trailing keycap slot bounds, and layout generation after label updates.
options.risk: Shortcut affordances become unreadable or detached from rows.

expectedEvidence.pass: Label and keycap rects do not overlap after resize.
expectedEvidence.fail: Keycap rect intersects label rect or uses stale row generation.

options.title: Submenu arrow and keycap hit targets are swapped
options.description: The visible submenu arrow appears where the keycap action is registered, or vice versa.
options.goal: Compare visual affordance bounds with semantic action kind and hit-test target.
options.risk: Pointer and keyboard affordances advertise different behavior.

expectedEvidence.pass: Arrow, keycap, and row semantic roles line up with hit targets.
expectedEvidence.fail: Arrow hit target does not open child route or keycap target is attached to submenu affordance.

options.title: Disabled parent action still opens submenu
options.description: A disabled parent action is greyed out but still opens its nested popup on hover.
options.goal: Prove disabled-state gating applies to hover, focus, keyboard, and nested route open paths.
options.risk: Disabled actions expose child commands that should be blocked.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/events.ts record --session <session> -- bun scripts/devtools/actions.ts inspect --session <session> --keep-open

expectedEvidence.pass: Disabled parent reports no child route activation.
expectedEvidence.fail: enabled=false parent opens child route or pending route request.

options.title: Nested menu anchor stays at old popup size
options.description: Opening a nested menu resizes the actions popup, but the nested anchor remains positioned for the old size.
options.goal: Compare popup resize generation with anchor generation and child menu bounds.
options.risk: Submenu placement becomes visibly detached after dynamic content changes.

expectedEvidence.pass: Anchor and menu bounds share the current resize generation.
expectedEvidence.fail: Child menu keeps pre-resize anchor coordinates.

options.title: Visible keycap triggers hidden nested action
options.description: Pressing a keycap shown on a top-level row triggers a hidden child action from a nested route.
options.goal: Inspect shortcut registry scope, route stack, visible row ownership, and dispatch target.
options.risk: Keyboard users execute an invisible command.

expectedEvidence.pass: Dispatch target belongs to visible active route.
expectedEvidence.fail: Shortcut binding resolves to hidden or stale child route.

options.title: Resizing host moves action focus to wrong row
options.description: When the popup host resizes with a submenu open, keyboard focus moves to a different row while highlight stays behind.
options.goal: Compare selected id, focused id, visible highlight bounds, and route stack before and after resize.
options.risk: Keyboard submission can activate a row other than the highlighted one.

expectedEvidence.pass: Focused id, selected id, and highlight bounds remain aligned after resize.
expectedEvidence.fail: Focus id and highlighted row diverge.

options.title: Prompt type switch keeps stale height
options.description: Switching from arg to div and back to choices keeps the previous prompt height.
options.goal: Prove prompt kind, content height, safe viewport, and resize generation reset on type switch.
options.risk: New prompt content renders clipped or with wasted space.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/inspect.ts --session <session> --start --show --main --bug "prompt type switch keeps stale height"

expectedEvidence.pass: Prompt kind change produces fresh resize generation and matching content bounds.
expectedEvidence.fail: Current prompt kind reports old container height or stale content bounds.

options.title: Async choices grow behind footer
options.description: Initial choices fit, but async-loaded choices grow behind the footer.
options.goal: Measure list viewport, footer occlusion, selected row visibility, and content height after async update.
options.risk: Visible rows are hidden under footer and keyboard selection is misleading.

expectedEvidence.pass: List safe viewport excludes footer and selected row is fully visible.
expectedEvidence.fail: Rows or selected item overlap footer bounds.

options.title: Main menu opens too tall after monitor change
options.description: After using an external monitor, the main menu opens huge on the laptop screen and clips bottom scripts.
options.goal: Compare window frame, active display visible frame, safe viewport, and list/footer bounds.
options.risk: Launcher is not usable after display topology changes.

expectedEvidence.pass: Window bounds fit current active display visible frame.
expectedEvidence.fail: Window height exceeds display visible frame or footer is offscreen.

options.title: Long div prompt is cut off without scrollbar
options.description: A long custom HTML report renders correctly at the top but lower content is unreachable.
options.goal: Prove div content height, viewport height, scroll container, and clipped node ids.
options.risk: Script output is silently inaccessible.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/inspect.ts --session <session> --start --show --main --bug "long div prompt cut off"

expectedEvidence.pass: Content declares scroll container and bottom content can be revealed.
expectedEvidence.fail: Content height exceeds viewport with no scrollable region.

options.title: Choices viewport does not shrink with prompt
options.description: Making the prompt shorter leaves the arg input taking space while choices do not resize correctly.
options.goal: Compare input bounds, list bounds, selected row bounds, and safe viewport after resize.
options.risk: Selected item is hidden or overlaps footer after resize.

expectedEvidence.pass: List viewport recalculates around input and footer.
expectedEvidence.fail: Selected row visibility uses pre-resize viewport.

options.title: Form tab focus goes below viewport
options.description: Tabbing through many form fields focuses lower fields without scrolling them into view.
options.goal: Inspect focused field id, field bounds, scroll top, and visible viewport.
options.risk: Keyboard users edit invisible fields.

expectedEvidence.pass: Focused field is fully visible after tab navigation.
expectedEvidence.fail: Focused field bounds are below safe viewport.

options.title: Path picker long names cause horizontal overflow
options.description: Long folder names make the list wider than the prompt and vertical scrolling becomes awkward.
options.goal: Measure row text bounds, container width, horizontal overflow, and selected row visibility.
options.risk: File navigation becomes unreadable or impossible with long paths.

expectedEvidence.pass: Long names truncate or wrap within container without horizontal overflow.
expectedEvidence.fail: Text bounds exceed list/container width.

options.title: Drop prompt accepted files list overflows
options.description: Dragging many files into a drop prompt grows the accepted list past the bottom of the window.
options.goal: Inspect accepted file count, list bounds, scroll region, footer overlap, and submit visibility.
options.risk: User cannot review or submit dropped files.

expectedEvidence.pass: Accepted files area is scrollable and submit remains visible.
expectedEvidence.fail: File rows extend beyond safe viewport or hide submit controls.

options.title: Template generated preview is clipped
options.description: A long generated preview is cut off while the editor remains visible.
options.goal: Measure editor, preview, divider, and scrollable preview content bounds.
options.risk: User approves a template without seeing the full generated output.

expectedEvidence.pass: Preview has independent scroll or visible full content.
expectedEvidence.fail: Preview content exceeds preview bounds with no scroll path.

options.title: Hotkey prompt shortcuts overlap instructions
options.description: Long key combos or many shortcuts overlap the shortcut list and instructions.
options.goal: Measure keycap text, instruction text, shortcut list rows, and overlap pairs.
options.risk: Shortcut registration UI becomes ambiguous.

expectedEvidence.pass: No overlap pairs among keycaps, instructions, and rows.
expectedEvidence.fail: Shortcut row or keycap overlaps instruction text.

options.title: DevTools inspects wrong note after rapid switch
options.description: Opening Notes then immediately switching notes sometimes leaves DevTools attached to the old note.
options.goal: Prove active note id, storage generation, selected note, and target identity agree.
options.risk: Automation edits or verifies the wrong note.

expectedEvidence.pass: Target identity and runtime activeNoteId match the visible note.
expectedEvidence.fail: Focused target or getState note id differs from visible/selected note.

options.title: Notes markdown preview stays stale
options.description: Script-updated note markdown does not refresh in preview until focus changes.
options.goal: Compare draft fingerprint, preview fingerprint, dirty state, and render generation.
options.risk: User sees obsolete note content.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/events.ts record --session <session> -- bun scripts/devtools/notes.ts inspect --session <session>

expectedEvidence.pass: Preview generation and fingerprint follow editor content update.
expectedEvidence.fail: Draft fingerprint changes while preview fingerprint stays old.

options.title: Notes preview scroll jumps after rich markdown render
options.description: Images, code blocks, or embedded ACP output cause the note preview to jump near the top.
options.goal: Measure scroll top before/after render generation and preview anchor preservation.
options.risk: User loses reading position while reviewing notes.

expectedEvidence.pass: Preview anchor or scroll top is preserved after render.
expectedEvidence.fail: Render generation changes and scroll top jumps without user input.

options.title: Notes split preview is clipped after resize
options.description: Resizing Notes in split mode clips the bottom of markdown preview.
options.goal: Compare editor pane, preview pane, footer, scroll metrics, and clipping chain.
options.risk: Note preview becomes partially unreachable.

expectedEvidence.pass: Preview pane has valid scroll height and no footer occlusion after resize.
expectedEvidence.fail: Preview content bottom is outside visible pane with no scroll recovery.

options.title: Embedded ACP steals Notes editor input
options.description: Typing in a note sometimes goes into an embedded ACP composer instead of the note editor.
options.goal: Inspect focus owner, editor selection, ACP composer focus, and keyboard route.
options.risk: Private note edits can be sent to the wrong surface.

expectedEvidence.pass: Keyboard owner matches intended editor/composer target.
expectedEvidence.fail: Focused note editor visually active but composer owns input.

options.title: Detached ACP forgets originating note
options.description: Detaching ACP from a note loses the note context it should answer with.
options.goal: Verify detached ACP parent note id, context chips, and attachment fingerprints.
options.risk: Agent replies without the context the user intended.

expectedEvidence.pass: Detached ACP retains originating note identity and context chip.
expectedEvidence.fail: Detached ACP has no parent note id or wrong context fingerprint.

options.title: ACP composer popup is misplaced after Notes scroll
options.description: A composer popup opened from a note selection appears in the wrong place after scrolling.
options.goal: Compare selected text rect, scroll offset, popup anchor, and popup bounds generation.
options.risk: User cannot tell what note selection will be attached.

expectedEvidence.pass: Popup anchor follows selected text rect after scroll.
expectedEvidence.fail: Popup uses pre-scroll or viewport-relative stale anchor.

options.title: Selected note section attaches wrong range
options.description: Attaching a selected note section sends the whole note or the wrong section.
options.goal: Compare selection range, attachment fingerprint, chip label, and redacted content length.
options.risk: Too much or wrong note content is sent to the agent.

expectedEvidence.pass: Attachment fingerprint and length match the selected section.
expectedEvidence.fail: Attachment length/fingerprint matches whole note or wrong range.

options.title: Streaming ACP reflows Notes and detaches popup
options.description: Streaming ACP output reflows the note preview and a composer popup drifts away from its anchor.
options.goal: Measure streaming generation, popup anchor, preview reflow, and layout generation alignment.
options.risk: User sends follow-up against a visually wrong context.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/events.ts record --session <session> -- bun scripts/devtools/notes.ts inspect --session <session> --open

expectedEvidence.pass: Popup repositions or closes cleanly when streaming reflows preview.
expectedEvidence.fail: Popup remains open with stale anchor after preview reflow.

options.title: Wrong ACP receives note context
options.description: With one embedded ACP and another detached ACP, attachments or messages route to the wrong agent.
options.goal: Prove target ACP id, originating note id, composer id, and attachment routing agree.
options.risk: User sends private or irrelevant context to the wrong chat.

expectedEvidence.pass: Context/message route target matches focused ACP and note identity.
expectedEvidence.fail: Attachment or message target id belongs to a different ACP.

options.title: Clipboard search shows blank matching rows
options.description: Search reports matches but several clipboard rows are blank and preview exposes unrelated content.
options.goal: Compare match evidence, row text fingerprints, preview source, and redaction state.
options.risk: Search UI hides matches or leaks clipboard content.

expectedEvidence.pass: Each matched row has visible redacted label and preview matches selected row.
expectedEvidence.fail: Matched rows are blank or preview source differs from selected row.

options.title: Launcher highlighted result jumps while typing
options.description: While searching for a script, the highlighted result jumps and source chip changes unexpectedly.
options.goal: Trace query, ranked ids, selected id, visible rows, and source filter state per input generation.
options.risk: User launches the wrong item by pressing Enter.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/events.ts record --session <session> -- bun scripts/devtools/text.ts measure --session <session> --main --strict

expectedEvidence.pass: Selected id remains valid for current ranked results and visible highlight.
expectedEvidence.fail: Selected id points to stale/offscreen result after input change.

options.title: Browser tabs and history duplicate URLs
options.description: Browser search mixes open tabs and history, showing duplicate URLs with inconsistent previews.
options.goal: Inspect source identity, URL normalization, duplicate groups, and selected preview source.
options.risk: User opens an old history item instead of the live tab.

expectedEvidence.pass: Duplicate URLs are grouped or clearly source-labeled and preview matches selected source.
expectedEvidence.fail: Same URL appears multiple times without stable source identity.

options.title: Drive filter shows local file results
options.description: Selecting a Drive source chip still shows local files and some previews show stale local content.
options.goal: Compare active source filters, row source ids, preview source id, and result count badges.
options.risk: User acts on a local file while intending cloud Drive results.

expectedEvidence.pass: Every visible row and preview belongs to active Drive source.
expectedEvidence.fail: Visible row source or preview source is local while Drive chip is active.

options.title: Emoji filter loses skin tone selection
options.description: Filtering emoji for thumbs produces mismatched rows and selected skin tone disappears after scrolling.
options.goal: Inspect query, emoji ids, skin tone state, selected id, and virtualized visible rows.
options.risk: User inserts the wrong emoji variant.

expectedEvidence.pass: Selected emoji id and skin tone persist through filter and scroll.
expectedEvidence.fail: Visible row metadata or selected skin tone resets after virtualization.

options.title: Process rows flicker and attach wrong metrics
options.description: Filtering processes for node causes rows to flicker and CPU numbers appear attached to the wrong process.
options.goal: Compare stable process ids, sort order, row ids, and metrics generation.
options.risk: User kills or inspects the wrong process.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/events.ts record --session <session> -- bun scripts/devtools/elements.ts snapshot --session <session> --main --strict

expectedEvidence.pass: Row identity and metrics generation stay aligned across refresh.
expectedEvidence.fail: CPU/memory values move to a different process id.

options.title: Settings search theme result is inconsistent
options.description: Searching settings for theme shows unrelated toggles or stale dark-mode state.
options.goal: Compare search match evidence, setting ids, visible labels, and actual setting values.
options.risk: User changes a setting unrelated to their search intent.

expectedEvidence.pass: Visible results include theme match evidence and current values.
expectedEvidence.fail: Rows lack theme evidence or value differs from persisted setting.

options.title: Source filter chips overflow and stale count remains
options.description: Adding multiple source filters hides chips in overflow and count badges remain active after removal.
options.goal: Inspect active chip ids, overflow chip ids, result count, and removal generation.
options.risk: User cannot tell which filters are active.

expectedEvidence.pass: Active filter model, visible/overflow chips, and count badge agree.
expectedEvidence.fail: Removed chip still affects count or hidden active filters lack disclosure.

options.title: Virtualized long list repeats rows after filter and scroll
options.description: Filtering works until scrolling; then rows repeat and the highlighted item points offscreen.
options.goal: Compare visible row ids, item indexes, selected id, scroll top, and query generation.
options.risk: User launches or selects the wrong item from a stale virtual row.

expectedEvidence.pass: Visible ids are unique and selected id is visible or revealable.
expectedEvidence.fail: Duplicate visible row ids or selected id outside filtered result set.

options.title: Private clipboard preview leaks after source switch
options.description: After previewing a private clipboard item, switching to files or browser history briefly shows the old clipboard preview.
options.goal: Trace selected item source, preview source, preview fingerprint, and surface switch generation.
options.risk: Sensitive clipboard content appears in the wrong search surface.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/events.ts record --session <session> -- bun scripts/devtools/elements.ts snapshot --session <session> --main --strict

expectedEvidence.pass: Surface switch clears prior preview or shows safe placeholder before new preview commits.
expectedEvidence.fail: Active surface/file item but preview still reports clipboard surface/item.

options.title: Window resurrects on disconnected display
options.description: Prompt says open but is invisible after unplugging an external monitor.
options.goal: Compare native window frame with currently attached display visible frames.
options.risk: User cannot recover prompt without restart or display workaround.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/inspect.ts --session <session> --show --bug "prompt open on disconnected display"

expectedEvidence.pass: Window intersects primary/current visible frame and screenshot metadata uses attached display.
expectedEvidence.fail: isVisible=true but window frame has zero intersection with all attached displays.

options.title: Display scale change offsets screenshots or hit areas
options.description: Moving between Retina and non-Retina displays leaves screenshot scale or clicks offset.
options.goal: Verify point/pixel coordinate conversion, backing scale, screenshot scale, and hit bounds.
options.risk: Automation clicks or visual proof target wrong pixels.

expectedEvidence.pass: Reported scales converge and screenshot pixel dimensions match point frame times scale.
expectedEvidence.fail: backingScaleFactor differs from screenshot scale or click center misses visible element.

options.title: Visible prompt does not own keyboard input
options.description: Prompt appears frontmost but typing goes into the previous app.
options.goal: Prove frontmost app, key window, first responder, and editable element ownership.
options.risk: User leaks typed content into another app.

expectedEvidence.pass: Frontmost pid, key window, first responder, and focused editable element all point to Script Kit.
expectedEvidence.fail: Screenshot shows prompt but ownsKeyboardInput=false or firstResponderElementId is null or wrong app.

options.title: Transparent overlay blocks visible control
options.description: A visible button does nothing because an invisible layer captures events.
options.goal: Identify topmost hit-test owner at the clicked coordinate and visual/hit bounds mismatch.
options.risk: Visible controls become unusable without an obvious UI clue.

expectedEvidence.pass: Topmost hit target at control center is the visible enabled control.
expectedEvidence.fail: Hit target resolves to transparent overlay or drag region above visible control.

options.title: Accessibility tree misses visible controls
options.description: VoiceOver cannot find visible interactive controls.
options.goal: Compare visual controls with AX nodes, roles, labels, frames, and focus order.
options.risk: Accessible navigation is broken despite correct pixels.

expectedEvidence.pass: Each visible interactive control maps to an AX node with role, label, enabled state, and frame.
expectedEvidence.fail: Visible enabled control has no AX node, no label, or mismatched AX frame.

options.title: High contrast or font scale clips UI
options.description: High contrast, dark mode, or larger text makes labels clipped or unreadable.
options.goal: Inspect effective appearance, font scale, contrast ratio, text bounds, and truncation.
options.risk: Accessibility settings make core UI unusable.

expectedEvidence.pass: Required text has acceptable contrast and no clipped labels at effective font scale.
expectedEvidence.fail: Increased contrast or font scale is active but contrast is below threshold or required label is clipped.

options.title: Notification steals focus and prompt never recovers
options.description: After a notification appears, Script Kit remains visible but stops accepting keyboard input.
options.goal: Capture before/during/after interruption focus and keyboard-owner state.
options.risk: Prompt looks active while input is dropped or routed elsewhere.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/events.ts record --session <session> -- bun scripts/devtools/keyboard.ts inspect --session <session> --main --strict

expectedEvidence.pass: Same first responder is restored and key events target expected editable element after interruption.
expectedEvidence.fail: isAppActive=true but ownsKeyboardInput=false or firstResponderElementId=null after notification.

options.title: Tray/menu popover leaves prompt operationally blocked
options.description: Prompt launched from menu bar looks active but clicks or shortcuts still belong to the menu loop.
options.goal: Detect native menu/modal-loop ownership, pointer capture, and shortcut routing owner.
options.risk: Prompt appears ready but cannot receive input.

expectedEvidence.pass: Menu state releases before prompt interaction and event ownership transfers to prompt.
expectedEvidence.fail: modalLoopActive or pointerCaptureOwner remains menu/popover while prompt is visible.

options.title: Prompt opens on wrong macOS Space
options.description: Prompt is visible in native state but appears on another desktop/Space or behind a fullscreen app.
options.goal: Compare window space id, active display, active Space, collection behavior, and screenshot target.
options.risk: User invokes prompt but cannot see it on the current workspace.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/inspect.ts --session <session> --show --bug "prompt opens on wrong Space"

expectedEvidence.pass: Prompt appears on invoking display active Space with matching screenshot metadata.
expectedEvidence.fail: isVisible=true but isOnActiveSpace=false or spaceId differs from frontmostSpaceId.

options.title: Screenshot says prompt is fine but user sees stale or occluded pixels
options.description: DevTools screenshot captures current prompt but the user-visible compositor shows old or occluded UI.
options.goal: Prove screenshot source, render epoch, window occlusion, and presented surface agree.
options.risk: False green proof from non-user-visible screenshots.
suggestedProofCommands: bun scripts/devtools/inspect.ts --session <session> --show --bug "screenshot disagrees with user-visible prompt"

expectedEvidence.pass: Screenshot provenance is explicit and render epoch matches presented user-visible surface.
expectedEvidence.fail: Screenshot is pre-compositor/window-only while window is occluded or render epoch is stale.
